title: Problem
description: A scheduling problem definition containing a time period, a global list of tasks, and one or more telescopes. Each telescope has its own location and optional constraints. An optional 'schedules' map can store computed solutions per telescope.

tasks.description: Global list of observation or calibration tasks. Each task can run on any telescope if its constraints allow.

telescopes.description: Set of telescopes/instruments, each with its own location and optional constraints.

schedules.description: Optional map of telescope ID to scheduled assignments. Present when saving solutions, omitted when loading input-only problems.

telescope.description: Telescope or instrument with its location and shared constraints that apply to all observations.

constraint.description: Instrument-level constraints (nighttime, moon altitude, etc.) that apply to all tasks scheduled on this telescope.

resource_type.description: Resource type for coalition scheduling (e.g. 'LST', 'MST', 'SST'). Defaults to 'telescope' if omitted.

assignment.description: A scheduled task assignment: which task runs during which interval. Field names match virolai Schedule serde output.

task.description: ID of the scheduled task (virolai serializes as 'task'; 'task_id' is accepted for backward compatibility).

interval.description: Numeric interval when this task is scheduled to run.
